command: sudo iptables -A INPUT -p udp --dport 9 -m string --algo bm --hex-string "|FFFFFFFFFFFF|" -m recent --name T1205_WOL --set -j LOG --log-prefix "T1205_WOL_SIG "
explanation: Detects Wake-on-LAN style magic packets (leading 6x 0xFF bytes) to use as a covert activation signal, consistent with T1205 traffic signaling patterns.
rationale_steps: Match application-specific WOL magic packet signature on UDP/9 to identify covert triggers.
Record source via recent list and log for anomaly detection without permitting access.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Valid iptables rule with correct flags, balanced quoting for hex-string and log-prefix, and proper use of string, recent, and LOG targets.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Effectively detects and logs UDP port 9 packets containing the WoL magic 0xFF sequence and tags them via recent, aligning with T1205 traffic signaling, though it only targets port 9 and does not anchor/validate the full WoL pattern.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with valid iptables flags and modules, though combining `-m recent --set` with `-j LOG` in a single rule is unusual (recent module typically precedes a separate action rule) and may not behave as expected on all iptables versions.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly targets UDP port 9 (WoL discard port) and matches the 6x 0xFF magic packet prefix using the string module with BM algorithm, logging matches as a detection/signaling trigger consistent with T1205 covert activation patterns, though a complete WoL magic packet detection would also check for the subsequent 16 repetitions of the target MAC address.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command follows correct iptables syntax, uses valid modules (string, recent, LOG), and properly formats the hex-string and log-prefix.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively identifies the 'magic' header of a Wake-on-LAN packet and uses the 'recent' module to track the signaling event, perfectly mirroring T1205 behavior.
